qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: MongoDB update operator injection via PATCH body in @feathersjs/mongodb
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same package and broad NoSQL-injection family, but different vulnerable code/root cause: this finding targets $-prefixed PATCH body keys becoming MongoDB update operators in _patch, whereas the advisory targets arbitrary JavaScript object id arguments over Socket.IO passing through getObjectId() into MongoDB query selection.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory is specifically Socket.IO clients sending object id arguments such as {$ne: null} to service methods, with those ids passing through getObjectId() into MongoDB queries. The manifest qualifying finding covers a distinct PATCH data-body update-operator injection.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-p9xr-7p9p-gpqx: arbitrary JavaScript objects supplied as Socket.IO service method id arguments pass through getObjectId() and land in MongoDB queries; {$ne: null} can match every document.

mongodb_patch_operator_injection/vulnerability_report.json: exploit path is PATCH request body $-prefixed keys passed by _patch into MongoDB update documents, not id object operators passed into query selection.
